mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of rising FEDFUNDS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand acceleration that outpaces supply constraints, lifting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial expansion following a supply shock, accelerating industrial production signals healing supply chains and margin recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-shock recovery regimes, positive month-over-month INDPRO growth coincides with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% sustained for more than one month indicates restrictive monetary policy that elevates borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive QoQ GDPC1 growth coincides with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in nonfarm payrolls signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE increases month-over-month and PAYEMS decreases month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of coincident months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals acute supply-side contraction and falling capacity utilization that squeezes corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines more than 1.0% month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.
